series_metadata.series_title: Medical Payment Dispute Committee (MPDC) Official Files.
series_metadata.series_description: These files contain the information submitted to the Medical Payment Dispute Committee (MPDC) for a conference. The MPDC is composed of representatives from the Legal Division, Medical Billing and Adjustments, Medical Policy and Support services. The files include copies of provider billing information concerning the provider’s dispute of the amount of overpayment made by the Bureau to the provider, a copy of Special Investigation Units /Report of Investigation, provider peer review of patient medical treatment notes with the appropriate medical billing code that should have been billed for services, spreadsheets of the provider overpayment, any evidence submitted at the Conference, any notes taken by the MCO Unit and the Official BWC MPDC Committee Order (Decision of the Committee).

retention_rules.disposition: Paper: Retain hard file in office for 2 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. Then Microfilm. (Hold paper file 120 days after microfilmed, for image quality assurance). Hold Microfilm in Office for 3 years, after 3 years move microfilm to offsite storage and store for 12 years. ; Electronic: Retain hard file in office for 2 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. Then Microfilm. (Hold paper file 120 days after microfilmed, for image quality assurance). Hold Microfilm in Office for 3 years, after 3 years move microfilm to offsite storage and store for 12 years. Retain CD’s or videos in office for 5 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. After 5 years the move the CD’s or videos with the microfilm to offsite storage and store for 12 years. ; Microform: Retain hard file in office for 2 years after MPDC Committee Order issued and/or conclusion of dispute and/or litigation. Then Microfilm. (Hold paper file 120 days after microfilmed, for image quality assurance). Hold Microfilm in Office for 3 years, after 3 years move microfilm to offsite storage and store for 12 years., Paper: Shred ; Electronic: Burn ; Microform: Burn
